directions: Soak miyeok 30-40min (til soft and hydrated). Drain and cut to bite-size.
Cut beef into bite-size pieces.
Heat sesame oil on pot, med high heat. Saute beef and miyeok, 3-5min.
Add water, salt, soy sauce, garlic if using. Stir and bring to boil.
Once boiling, lower heat to med or med low. Cook covered 20-30 min.
The longer you cook the better. Add water/seasoning to taste as you cook.

recipes.name: tteokguk
recipes.label: tteokguk [떡국]

items.name: beef_chuck_stew
items.note: can also use flank steak or brisket, chop into small pieces

items.name: rice_cake_sliced
items.note: or is it supposed to be 1lb???? unknown; do by eye

items.name: dried_seaweed_sheet
items.note: crushed or cut to thin strips

directions: Bring water to a boil in a heavy pot over high heat. Add beef and garlic, cook 5 min.
Turn heat down to med, cover, and cook for 20-25 min til beef is tender.
Add rice cakes, soup soy sauce, salt, pepper, sesame oil. Cook 7-8 min til rice cakes are soft.
Gently fry egg, slice into thin slices.
Serve and add garnish.

recipes.name: sujebi
recipes.label: sujebi [수제비]
recipes.groceryList: (1 potato, 1/2 onion, 1 carrot, 1 zucchini, 2 garlic)
directions: Knead dough til smooth (10-15min) and let rest at least 1 hr. The longer the better.
Kelp, anchovy, green onion white part (opt), water, med high heat for 10 min. Remove and set aside kelp. Boil 10 more min, remove everyone.
Add potato, onion, zucchini, carrot, garlic. Boil 10-15 min med high heat.
Cut kelp into bite size pieces. Add kelp, fish sauce, soup soy sauce, salt if needed to taste.
Tear dough thinly into boiling water.
Close lid and cook for a couple min til dough floating.
Add green onion green part and sesame oil at the end.

recipes.name: korean_pancakes
recipes.label: korean pancakes [부침개]
directions: Kimchi pancakes:
Makes ~5 pancakes.
Med to med high heat, 1/2 generous cup of batter + 1 tsp oil per pancake.

recipes.name: steamed_egg
recipes.label: steamed egg [계란찜]
directions: Whisk egg, water, shrimp. Pour into ramekins
Fill water up to half ramekin height. Bring to boil.
Put in ramekins, set to med low heat for 8 min.
Stir, add gochugaru, and keep on for 5ish more min (until set)

recipes.name: soy_sauce_egg
recipes.label: soy sauce egg
directions: Soft boil eggs. (Place in boiling water for 7-8ish min).
Put eggs in sauce, try to get all covered. Soak overnight. Eat within 5 days.

recipes.name: rolled_egg
recipes.label: rolled egg [계란마리]
directions: Beat eggs, add filling.
On med low heat (smaller pan for thicker roll!), add oil, add 1/2 of egg mix and swirl to cover pan.
Cook until bottom is done, until top is set but wet.
Fold over a bit of one side (~1 inch). Fold over again.
Move the folded side to the edge, add rest of egg mix to open space on pan.
Fold over again once the newly added mix is similarly set but wet. Remove from heat, let cool, then slice.

recipes.name: kimchi
recipes.label: kimchi [김치]

items.name: salted_shrimp
items.note: try decreasing... probably should be 2 tsp

directions: For cabbage kimchi:
Chop napa into bite size pieces.
Soak in salt water for at least 2 hrs. Should be limp!
Drain and rinse cabbage a few times.
Toss with sauce. Use about 1/8c more water to rinse out the sauce bowl and add to kimchi.
For radish kimchi:
Cut radish. Rinse and dry.
Toss in salt and sugar. Let sit for 30 min.
Mix sauce ingredients with 1/3 cup of the juice from the drained radish.

recipes.name: lotus_root_jorim
recipes.label: lotus root jorim [연근조림]

items.name: honey
items.note: or sugar. to sub for 3/4c rice syrup?

directions: Slice lotus roots into 1/4 inch thick discs. Soak in cold water for 30 min.
Boil water in a pot, add vinegar, and blanch lotus root for 5 min, uncovered. Rinse in cold water and strain.
Heat up oil in a pot. Med heat? Stir fry the roots for a few min til softer and translucent.
Add water, garlic, soy sauce. Stir and cover, heat to low, cook for 40 min. Occasionally open cover to turn roots over for even cooking. Add more water if necessary.
Add sweetener and stir. Cover and simmer for another 20 min.
Open and turn heat to med high. Carefully stir and turn over roots, braise for 10-12min, til almost all liquid evaporated and roots are jelly like.
Remove from heat, stir in sesame oil, sprinkle with sesame seeds. Can store in fridge for up to 2 weeks.

recipes.name: soybean_sprout_muchim
recipes.label: soybean sprout muchim [콩나물무침]
directions: Rinse sprouts in cold water, remove rotten beans or floating skins.
Place 1 cup water + bean sprouts in medium pot. Cover, bring to boil over high heat, cook for 3-4 min and DO NOT open lid.
Drain quickly, cool. Shock sprouts in cold water. Toss with remaining ingredients.

recipes.name: seaweed_muchim
recipes.label: seaweed muchim [미역무침]

items.name: cucumber
items.note: optional, thinly sliced. can also do radish

directions: Soak miyeok in cold water (prob 10-12 min, check package) or just til soft. Rinse, drain, and cut. Blanch miyeok in boiling water, drain and rinse and cool and squeeze out water.
Mix all sauce ingredients. Toss all ingredients.

recipes.name: cucumber_kimchi
recipes.label: cucumber kimchi [오이김치]

items.name: salt
items.note: coarse salt; use less if table salt

directions: Quarter cucumber and cut to 1.5" long pieces. Salt evenly and let sit ~30min.
Drain cucumbers but do not rinse. Add onion and chives. Toss well in seasoning.

recipes.name: cucumber_muchim
recipes.label: cucumber muchim [오이무침]
recipes.groceryList: (0.5lb cucumber (~1 avg), 1 green onion, 1 garlic)
directions: Toss cucumbers gently in salt. Let sit 15 min.
Drain excess water but don't squeeze hard. Mix well with all remaining ingredients.

recipes.name: sweet_potato_stem_namul
recipes.label: sweet potato stem namul [건고구마순나물]
recipes.groceryList: (dried sweet potato stem, 1 garlic, 1 green onion)
directions: Soak stems til soft (6hrs?). Cut into bite size length. Heat up on greased pan.
Add soy sauce, salt, saute a bit. Add water a little and let boil.
Add garlic, green onions, sesame oil, saute. Add perilla powder at end.

recipes.name: gosari_namul
recipes.label: gosari namul [고사리나물]
recipes.groceryList: (gosari, 1 garlic, 1 green onion)
directions: In medium pot, gosari and 4-5 cup water. Boil covered over med heat til tender (~30-40min). Turn heat off and let cool in cooking water. Rinse in cold water and drain.
Cut into 3-4" pieces. Remove tough stem ends if needed.
Mix gosari with soy sauce, sugar, garlic. Let sit 10 min.
Saute in skillet with 1 tbsp oil for 4-5 min med heat. Pour in water/broth, cook for few more min stirring occasionally til water mostly gone.
Add rest of ingredients. Stir well. Take off heat.

recipes.name: spinach_namul
recipes.label: spinach namul [시금치나물]
recipes.groceryList: (1 bunch spinach, 1 green onion, 1 garlic)

items.name: soy_sauce_soup
items.note: or 2 tsp gochujang + 2 tsp soy sauce

directions: Rinse spinach. Blanch in salted boiling water til wilted (20-30sec). Quickly remove and shock in icy cold water.
Drain and squeeze excess water gently. Cut into 3" length
Toss and mix well with rest of ingredients.
Store 3-4 days in fridge.

recipes.name: peanut_jorim
recipes.label: peanut jorim [땅콩조림]
directions: Bring peanuts + 2 cup water to a boil. Boil for 5 min. Drain.
Return peanuts to pot. Add soy sauce, mirin, sugar, corn syrup, water/broth.
Bring to boil. Reduce meat to med and gently boil uncovered til most sauce evaporated (~20-25min).
Stir occasionally but monitor to prevent burning. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Keep in fridge up to 2-3 weeks.

recipes.name: potato_jorim
recipes.label: potato jorim [감자조림]
recipes.groceryList: (1.5 lb (~3 med) potato, 1 carrot?, 1/2 any bell pepper, 1/4 onion)
directions: Cut potatoes into 1" cubes. Cut other veg into big chunks.
Mix all braising ingredients except sesame oil and sesame seeds, stir well
In non-stick pan/pot, saute potatoes over med heat 4-5 min, stir occasionally.
Add sauce and bring to boil over high heat. Add carrots.
Cover, reduce heat to med, cook till potatoes almost cooked thru (~5-6 min), firm but not mushy.
Stir in peppers and onion. Boil uncovered til sauce reduced and thick (~3min). Stir in sesame oil and seeds.
Keeps in fridge 2-3 days.

recipes.name: eomuk_bokkeum
recipes.label: eomuk bokkeum [어묵 볶음]
recipes.groceryList: (1/2 carrot or 1/4 red bell, 1/4 green bell, 1/4 onion, 1 garlic)
directions: Optionally cook fish cakes in boiling water for 30 sec then drain if you want to remove excess oil.
Thinly slice veg. Combine and mix all sauce ingredients well.
Non stick pan on med high heat, cook veg for 1-2 min or til silghtly soft.
Reduce heat to med, cook fish cake ~2 min. Stir in sauce well, cook ~2 min.
Veg options are to liking--can do green onions, chili peppers, etc
Keep 4-5 days in fridge.

recipes.name: steamed_shishito
recipes.label: steamed shishito [꽈리고추찜]
recipes.groceryList: (10oz shishito, 2 garlic, 2 green onion)
directions: Trim, rinse, and drain peppers. Toss peppers in flour to coat.
Bring water in steamer to "high simmer." Add peppers, cover, and steam for ~5 min or til tender. Don't overcook or will get mushy!
Toss peppers in sauce.
Keeps for few days in fridge.

recipes.name: myulchi_bokkeum
recipes.label: myulchi bokkeum [멸치]
directions: Cook anchovies 2-3 min over med heat in pan with oil. Turn off heat. Set anchovies aside on plate.
Mix well all sauce ingredients except sesame oil and seeds. Pour into pan you were using, and boil over med heat til bubbly and silghtly thick (~2-3min).
Add anchovies back in and optional jalapeno. Stir well til anchovies coated well. Garnish with sesame oil and sesame seeds.
Keeps in fridge up to a week.

recipes.name: kung_pao
recipes.label: kung pao
directions: Marinate, cook high heat, and set aside protein.
1 tsp oil into wok med heat, cook scallions, ginger, and garlic with the peppercorn powder on low. Cook for 1-2 min.
Add protein back in. Turn heat up and stir fry with the sauce for 1 min. Add peanuts, stir quick, and serve.

recipes.name: beef_rice_bowl
recipes.label: beef rice bowl
directions: Heat a wok on high heat, add oil. Add beef, break up large chunks, cook until slightly browned.
Add onion, garlic, carrots, Shaoxing wine, and turn heat down to med. Cook until the onion is translucent, and then add chicken stock.
Bring to boil and lower heat to a simmer. Add sauces, sesame oil, pepper. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir cornstarch + water mix and drizzle in while mixing. Simmer til thick enough to coat spoon.

recipes.name: broccoli_tofu_stirfry
recipes.label: broccoli tofu stirfry
directions: Marinate protein.
Fry tofu if using: Med heat, 1 tbsp oil, about 5 min per side. Set aside. (Don't really need to make too crispy.. it'll get soaked in sauce anyway)
Wok med heat, cook garlic in 1 tsp oil for 30 sec.
Add broccoli, cook for 30 sec. Add wine around edge of wok.
Add tofu and sauce and walnuts.

recipes.name: chili_oil
recipes.label: chili oil
directions: Keep at 225-250F for 30 min. Pour over gochugaru.
Makes 1.5 cups of oil? Store in fridge for 6 months.
Makes 393g of oil (7-8g per tbsp. ~50 tbsp total)

recipes.name: dan_bing
recipes.label: dan bing
directions: Makes 2 pancakes
Mix flour, salt, water with spatula until sticky dough forms, ~2-3 min.
Coat dough with 1/2 tsp veg oil. Cover with plastic wrap. Rest for 1 hr.
Roll out with rolling pin into almost translucent.
Heat skillet or frying pan over med high heat.
Add 1/2 tsp oil. Lift rolled out dough, place evenly in pan.
Crack 1 egg into center of dough. Lightly spread egg around over top of dough.
Drizzle another 1/2 tsp oil on top.
Flip and cook this for 10 sec.
Flip again. Let cook for another min or until golden brown.
Roll each pancake up, slice the rolls, and top with sauce.

recipes.name: lamb_cumin_noodles
recipes.label: lamb cumin noodles
directions: Combine flour, water, salt. Knead for 20 min til you can stretch noodle to several inches without breaking. Rest for 1-2 hours.
Marinate lamb.
Toast cumin seeds, grind, and mix spices into rest of sauce.
Prepare ginger + scallions. Prepare garlic + jalapeno. Prepare cut cabbage. Prepare onions.
Roll out noodle dough into 10 inch rect and cut into one-inch pieces. Should be spaghettic thickness (shouldnt be transparent).
Pull each piece lengthwise and slap/pound onto flat surface.
On med heat wok, 3 tbsp veg oil with ginger + scallions for 1 min.
Then add garlic + jalapeno and cook for 30 sec.
Add lamb til it browns.
Add onion and sauce. At this point, put noodles + cabbage in boiling water for 90 sec to 2 min.
Add noodles + cabbage to wok and mix til combined.

recipes.name: singapore_noodles
recipes.label: singapore noodles

items.name: egg
items.note: whisked with a pinch of salt

directions: Soak noodles in boiling water for 3-4 min. Drain and cut into thirds.
Heat oil on wok on med heat. Add salt (pepper too if wanted) and mix. Cook chicken/tofu until browned, then put onto plate.
Cook onion on med-high heat 3 min. Add all other veg, cook for couple min til wilted.
Make well in center of veg, add egg and scramble. Mix everything, then put onto plate.
Add oil, cook noodles on med heat 2-3 min.
Spread out noodles, add veg, add chicken/tofu, sprinkle dry spices. Stir until all combined.
Pour sauces in, toss and cook for 1-2 min.

recipes.name: mapo_tofu
recipes.label: mapo tofu
directions: (1) Peppercorn, ginger, garlic. (2) Pork. (3) The beans. (4) Cornstarch. (5) Chili oil. (6) Tofu.
Heat oil on wok, med heat.
Add ground peppercorns and stir occasionally for 30 sec. Add ginger. After 1 min, add garlic.
After 1 min, turn heat to high and add ground pork. Fry til cooked thru.
Add spicy bean sauce, stir in well. Add beans and cook for 30 sec.
Add 2/3 c water and stir. Simmer for 1 min.
Mix cornstarch with 1/4 c water. Add mix to sauce and stir.
Add chili oil, stir, and add tofu. Cook for 3-5 min.

recipes.name: chicken_black_bean_stirfry
recipes.label: chicken black bean stirfry
recipes.groceryList: (12oz chicken thigh, ginger, 3 garlic, 1 bell pepper, mushroom?, cabbage?, 1 carrot, 1 onion)
directions: Marinate chicken.
2 tbsp oil on med heat. Cook beans, garlic, chicken til chicken is opaque.
Add veg and stir fry for 1 min.
Add sugar, ginger powder, wine, salt. Stir fry.

recipes.name: cumin_lamb
recipes.label: cumin lamb

items.name: oil
items.note: optional if lamb is a fattier cut

directions: Cut lamb. Marinate for 30min.
Dry toast cumin seeds on med heat. Set aside.
High heat wok. Once hot, 2 tbsp oil and lamb.
Sear meat until crispy and brown.
Add rest of ingredients and toss til just combined (greens will be wilted). Serve.

recipes.name: eggplant_with_garlic_sauce
recipes.label: eggplant with garlic sauce
recipes.groceryList: (4 eggplant, ginger, 6 garlic, 1/2lb pork)
directions: (1) Eggplants. (2) Pork. (3) Ginger, garlic. (4) Sauce.
Cut eggplants. Soak fully submerged in salt water for 15 min. Drain and pat dry.
Coat eggplants in cornstarch by hand.
1 tbsp oil in nonstick skillet med high heat. Spread eggplants without overlap. (if at any point, too smokey, turn to med heat)
Cook eggplants one side at a time til charred and soft (~8-10min total). Transfer eggplants to plate.
Remaining oil, cook pork for til MOSTLY not pink. Add garlic and ginger and cook til soft.
Add all eggplants back, pour sauce over, quickly stir a few times til all coated and sauce is thick.

recipes.name: mongolian_beef
recipes.label: mongolian beef
directions: Marinate beef for 1hr in oil, soy sauce, cornstarch. If dry after marinating, add 1tbsp water.
Lightly coat beef in cornstarch.
Wok high heat, sear beef for 1 min, then 30 sec on other side. Set to side, try to let oil drain. (Cook in batches if 1+ lb of meat)
Drain oil from wok, leave 1 tbsp behind. Turn heat to med-high. Add ginger and dried peppers, if using, and garlic after 15 sec. After 10 sec, and add soy sauce and water.
Bring sauce to a simmer, add the brown sugar, and stir until dissolved.
Let simmer for ~2min and slowly stir in cornstarch slurry until the sauce coats back of spoon.

Add beef and green onions. Toss everything together for 30 sec. Should be almost no liquid, sauce should be clinging to the beef. If you still have sauce, increase the heat slightly and stir until thickened.

recipes.name: tomato_egg
recipes.label: tomato egg
recipes.groceryList: (green onion, 500g cherry tomato or 4 roma?)
directions: (1) Whisked egg, salt, pepper, sesame oil, shaoxing. (2) Tomatoes, scallions. (3) Sugar, salt, water.
Cut tomatoes into small wedges. Chop scallion.
Whisk egg with salt, pepper, sesame oil, shaoxing.
Wok on med heat til just smoking.
2 tbsp oil and immediately add egg. Scramble and immediately remove. Set aside.
1 tbsp oil, wok to high heat, add tomatoes and scallions. Stir fry for a min.
Add sugar, salt, water. Add more water if stove usually gets too hot and liquids get cook down.
Add cooked eggs, mix everything, cover and cook 1-2 min til tomatoes soft.
Uncover, stir fry til sauce is thickened/water is reduced to liking.

recipes.name: ginger_chicken
recipes.label: ginger chicken
directions: SMASH garlic, ginger, and white parts of scallions.
Spread oil around perimeter of wok. Heat it til it just starts smoking.
Add smashed ginger slices, fry for 15 sec.
Spread the chicken pieces in a single layer in the wok. Sear for 45 sec.
Flip them and fry other side for another 30 sec.
Add in white parts of scallions, garlic, and shallots. Stir-fry over high heat, mix everything another 30 sec.
Add Shaoxing, and stir fry another 20 sec. Add in chicken stock, sugar, soy sauces, pepper, and oyster sauce.
Cover and continue to cook on high heat for 5 minutes.
Remove cover, and cook another 7 min to reduce liquid.
Mix in green parts of scallions, and then immediately stir in cornstarch water mix to thicken sauce.

recipes.name: bon_bon_chicken
recipes.label: bon bon chicken
directions: Make sauce. Shred chicken. Slice green onion. Toss. Good with 100g rice :)

recipes.name: pad_see_ew
recipes.label: pad see ew
directions: Soak noodles. Make sauce. Separate broccoli stems and leaves. Cut leaves into halves or thirds. Cut stems into bite size, thinner pieces.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in wok, high heat.
Add garlic, cook 15 sec. Add chicken, cook til mosty changed from pink to white.
Add broccoli stems, cook til chicken is almost cooked through.
Add broccoli leaves, cook ti just wilted.
Push everything to one side, crack egg in and scramble. Remove everything onto a plate.
Heat another 1 tbsp on high heat.
Add noodles, let crisp. Add sauce. Toss together. Add chicken and broccoli back in, toss, serve.

recipes.name: pad_thai
recipes.label: pad thai
directions: Boil noodles. Rinse under cold water.
Mix fish sauce, sugar, rice vinegar, water, chili powder.
Heat skillet, add oil, cook garlic.
Cook tofu, add noodles, cook for 30 seconds.
Cook egg, add sauce.
Stir in peanut.

recipes.name: vague_thai_curry
recipes.label: vague thai curry
directions: Stir fry paste in oil. Add 2/3rds of can of coconut milk.
Add meat and cook. Add rest of coconut milk.
Add vegetables, wait until cooked.
Add cornstarch to thicken and serve over rice.

recipes.name: chicken_adobo
recipes.label: chicken adobo
directions: Marinate chicken for at least 20 min (up to overnight).
Heat 1 tbsp oil on skillet over high heat.
Remove chicken from marinade (reserve marinade) and onto pan.
Sear both sides til brown (~1 min per side). Don't cook all the way through. Put chicken aside.
Heat remaining oil (or use chicken grease!), cook garlic and onion for 1.5 min.
Add marinade, water, sugar, black pepper. Bring it to simmer then turn heat down to med high. Simmer 5 min.
Add chicken smooth side down.
Simmer uncovered for 20~25 min (no need to stir), turning chicken at ~15 min, until sauce reduces down to thick jam-like syrup.

recipes.name: vietnamese_bun
recipes.label: vietnamese bun
recipes.groceryList: (1.5lb chicken thigh, 4 garlic, 1 carrot, 1 cucumber, romaine, cilantro)
directions: Marinate chicken for 30min-1hr.
Combine nuoc cham ingredients and stir until sugar completely dissolved. Adjust if desired.
Boil, drain, rinse vermicelli.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in skilllet, med high heat (6/10).
Sear chicken 3 min each side. Pour in marinade and some water, then cover and cook for 7-10 min on med low (4/10) heat. Set aside.
Slice chicken thighs and add to salad.
Combine all ingredients. Serve with nuoc cham sauce.
(Original chicken cooking instructions: 2 tbsp oil med high heat (8/10). Sear chicken for 3 min one side, 5 min other (or til cooked thru). Set aside.)

recipes.name: banh_mi
recipes.label: banh mi
recipes.groceryList: (1 carrot, 1 daikon, 10.24oz (0.64lb) chicken thigh, 2 garlic, take bake baguette, cilantro, jalapeno)
directions: Toss carrot and radish with sugar, salt, vinegar. Set aside 1 hr, stir occasionally.
Marinate chicken 1-4 hrs.
Heat 2 tsp oil in skilllet, med high heat (6/10).
Sear chicken 3 min each side. Pour in marinade and some water, then cover and cook for 7-10 min on med low (4/10) heat.
Mix sauce. Assemble sandwich.

recipes.name: one_pot_thai_curry
recipes.label: one pot thai curry
recipes.groceryList: (1 onion, 1 jalapeno?, 2 green bell, 1.333lb chicken thigh, 1 can coconut milk, basil)
directions: (1) Onion and veggies. (2) Chicken. (3) Curry paste, rice. (4) Broth, coconut milk. (5) Basil.
Large skillet (dutch oven), heat 1 tbsp oil over med high heat. Saute onion + veggies for 5+min (til soft).
Add chicken and cook til no longer pink (~3min).
Push mixture to side of pan. Add 1 tbsp oil, curry paste, and rice. Saute for 30 sec (this is to help spices bloom!)
Quickly add coconut milk.
Simmer covered on low heat (3/10) for 35 min. Stir in basil during last few min, and serve.

recipes.name: vague_peanut_butter_noodle
recipes.label: vague peanut butter noodle
recipes.groceryList: (1 garlic, 1 bell pepper, 1 imit crab, cilantro?)
directions: Mix sauce ingredients.
Cook noodle.
Heat oil in skillet, high heat. Stir fry garlic and bell pepper til softer.
Add noodle and sauce. Stir fry for a bit. Crack in egg and whisk.
Add crab and peanut, quick cook, and serve.

recipes.name: california_roll_noodle
recipes.label: california roll noodle
recipes.groceryList: (1 imit crab, 1 cucumber, 2 avocado)
directions: Cook noodle. (If using dry udon, boil water on high? or any heat? Add noodle and cook in boiling water for 4 min, then drain.)
Cut vegetables. Add noodles. Toss in sugar, salt, and a splash of rice vinegar. Combine

recipes.name: eggy_beef_bowl
recipes.label: eggy beef bowl
directions: https://omnivorescookbook.com/cantonese-ground-beef-rice-eggs/

recipes.name: steamed_meat_buns
recipes.label: steamed meat buns
recipes.groceryList: (1 bison meat, ginger, 1 sm onion)

items.name: water
items.note: room temp; to sub cold milk

ingredients.category: filling. opt can add 1/2 tbsp hoisin + 1/2 tbsp oyster in addition

directions: Makes 10 big or 20 small buns.
To make dough:
Mix flour, yeast, sugar, salt, oil. Add toom temp water. (slowly add more water if too dry. probably more like 180ml but up to  200ml). Mix til rough dough.
Cover and let rest 15 min. Knead 10-15min more. If hard to knead, just give it a rest.
Do one more 15min rest before shaping. Divide dough into smaller pieces, rolling into very smooth tall balls (smooth top with milk/water if desired).

Place each ball on parchment paper. Let rise only once, to 50% larger (don't let double in size!) Done proving when poking dough leaves indent that bounces back slowly (not enough if bounce back fast). (very roughly 30min-1hr room temp)
Bring steamer water to boil, lower the heat to MEDIUM. Place buns 1 inch apart, close lid and crack open about a 1/4 inch gap.
Steam 15 min for no filling, 18 min for filling.
After steaming, turn off heat. Do NOT open lid and let sit for 5 min.
To make filling:
Med heat wok, cook ginger + onion til soft.
Add meat and cook til pale and opaque (but NOT brown or crispy).
Add sauces. Cook off until liquids are evaporated.
Add cornstarch + water. Cook 30 sec. Let filling cool off heat.

recipes.name: vegan_mac_n_cheese
recipes.label: vegan mac & cheese
directions: Cashew version: Cook pasta. Blend everything til smooth. Combine.
Non-Cashew version:
In small saucepan, heat oil over med. Add flour, whisk to incorporate.
Continue whisking, cook for 2 min (mixture will be bubbly). Add milk slowly, whisking constantly.
Add spices. Turn heat to low, cook til thick (~8min) stirring frequently.
Remove from heat, add yeast, stir til everything is incorporated and smooth. Taste for flavor.

recipes.name: refried_beans
recipes.label: refried beans
directions: Cook garlic in oil med heat for 4-5 min, then smash in pan.
Add everything except lime juice, cook for ~5 min.
Take off heat, mash, add lime juice and water as needed.

recipes.name: hummus
recipes.label: hummus
directions: Put everything in food processor, puree til smooth. Blend with 2 ice cubes, optional.
Store covered in fridge for up to a week.

recipes.name: pesto
recipes.label: pesto
directions: Makes 1 cup (good for use with 12oz pasta)
Blend. Store covered in fridge up to a week.
Cook pasta. Cook chicken. Combine.

recipes.name: stuffing
recipes.label: stuffing
recipes.groceryList: (baguette, 3 celery, 2 carrot, 5 mushroom, 1 onion, 2 garlic)
directions: Add 2 tbsp olive oil on nonstick pan, med heat. Add vegetables, black pepper, parsley, and salt. Cook vegetables until soft.
Add bread. Mix and add 1.5c water, soy sauce, and spices.
Turn heat to low and let cook until bread soft and water dissolved.

recipes.name: deviled_eggs
recipes.label: deviled eggs

items.name: vinegar_apple_cider
items.note: or sub with white vinegar or PICKLE JUICE

directions: To hardboil eggs:
Place eggs in pot, cover with cold water.
Put pot on stove, high heat. Once water starts boiling, turn off heat and keep pot on hot stove, with cover on. Let sit for 10 min.
Place eggs in ice water bath for 14 min. Peel.
Cut eggs in half. Scoop out yolk. Mash yolk, add other filling ingredients, and mix well.

recipes.name: crab_salad
recipes.label: crab salad
directions: Mix. add chopped pickles if you want

recipes.name: crab_dip
recipes.label: crab dip
directions: Blend cream cheese with milk. Add crab meat, onion, horseradish, salt, pepper (spice to taste).
Bake 15 min at 375F. Serve with potato chips.

recipes.name: salmon_rillette
recipes.label: salmon rillette
recipes.groceryList: (1 shallot, 1lb king salmon, 3oz smoked salmon, chives)
directions: Combine wine + shallots in large saucepan, bring to a boil. Simmer on med-low heat til shallots are tender (~2min)

Add fresh salmon pieces and poach til just barely opaque (~2-3min). Remove salmon from wine and immediately drain them on a paper towel. Strain the wine, reserving the shallots. Place salmon and shallots in fridge to cool completely.
Combine poached salmon pieces, cooked shallots, smoked salmon, chives, mayo, lemon juice in a stainless steel bowl. Gently stir til well mixed, don't overmix, season with salt pepper.
Can be made 1 day ahead and covered and chilled.

recipes.name: squid_luau
recipes.label: squid luau
recipes.groceryList: (1lb spinach, squid, 1 can coconut milk)

items.name: spinach
items.note: sub fresh luau leaves--should be about 1lb

directions: Rinse leaves, remove stems, cut into 1 inch pieces.
Bring 2 cup water to boil.
Add leaves and cook til tender, reducing heat to simmer. (If squid is raw, cook now with leaves, simmering for 1 hr)
Drain leaves. Stir in coconut milk, salt, squid bits (if cooked separately).
Add sugar and continue to cook over low to medium heat, stirring constantly until the leaves have absorbed most of the liquid.

recipes.name: syrniki
recipes.label: syrniki
directions: Beat egg and sugar until smooth.
Add rest of ingredients and mix.
Scoop out 1/4 cups of batter and fry on med low heat.

recipes.name: vegan_pancakes
recipes.label: vegan pancakes
directions: Mix dry ingredients. Separately mix wet and let sit to curdle.
Combine the two and mix, let sit for 5-15 min.
Cook 1/4 cup batter each over lightly oiled pan, med heat (5-6/10). Flip once top bubbles.

recipes.name: chia_pudding
recipes.label: chia pudding
directions: for thick chia: 2 tbsp for every 1/2 c (4g chia for every 20ml milk)
for thinner chia: 3 tbsp for every 1 c (3g chia for every 20ml milk)

recipes.name: dutch_baby
recipes.label: dutch baby
directions: Preheat oven 425 and put skillet inside. (9x5 pan works)
Blend egg, flour, milk, sugar, and seasonings. Ideally ingredients are room temp!!!
Put butter in pan, tilt to coat with butter.
Add batter and return to oven for 15 to 20 min, until puffed and golden brown.

recipes.name: fluffy_pancakes
recipes.label: fluffy pancakes
directions: Mix butter and milk and vanilla. Beat egg, then lightly beat in butter mix.
Sift dry ingredients. Create a well in the dry ingredients. Add wet to dry and mix just enough.
Lightly oiled pan on med heat, 1/4 c batter each pancake.

recipes.name: banana_pancakes
recipes.label: banana pancakes
directions: Mix dry, mix wet. About 1/2c batter per pancake on med heat.

recipes.name: breakfast_patties
recipes.label: breakfast patties
directions: Mix all spices. Add spices to pork. Nice to let sit for a few hours to marinate.
Med heat non stick 5 min each side.

recipes.name: protein_pancakes
recipes.label: protein pancakes
directions: For more protein, try replacing some of the flour with more protein powder... up to half of the flour.
Mix milk, vinegar, and vanilla. Let curdle.
Mix all dry, then add curdled wet ingredients. Mix gently (don't overmix!) Let sit 5 min.
Med heat non stick few min each side til golden.

recipes.name: tempeh_egg_baguette
recipes.label: tempeh egg baguette
directions: Enjoy :)

recipes.name: biscuits
recipes.label: biscuits
directions: Make buttermilk by replacing 1tbsp w vinegar. So, 1/16 of liquid amount is vinegar, 15/16 of liquid amount is milk.
Stir dry together. Cut in the frozen fat (use a veg peeler!). Make well in center and stir in liquid.
Knead about 10 times. (Do the book fold thing ~3 times)
Roll to 1 inch thick and cut out (dont twist!!) or slice with knife, and clean cutter between each cutting.
Have biscuits right next to each other so the sides touch (so they stay soft). Optional, brush tops with buttermilk.
Bake at 425F for 15 min.
(To freeze: Cut, dont brush tops, cover with plastic wrap and freeze. Cook at 475F from frozen (brush now if you want) for 7 min, then keep door closed, turn oven off, leave for 5 more min.)

recipes.name: pie_crust
recipes.label: pie crust
directions: For one 9-inch pie crust:
Combine flour, salt, sugar. (Food processor: pulse til well combined)
Add the butter, half at a time. (Pulse 8 times, add, then pulse 6 times)
Slowly add ice water + vinegar mix. (Pulse several times each time you add.)
Should be crumbly dough that holds together when you squish it.
Form dough into a disk, wrap and chill in fridge for at least 30min-1 hour.. Work dough just enough to bring together, too much will make it tough.
Remove from fridge and let sit for 10-15 min. Roll out once soft.
Freeze til ready to cook.
To blindbake:
Freeze the un-cooked pie crust for at least 30 minutes, preferably an hour, before blind-baking.
Bake at 350°F for 45-50 minutes if making a crust for a pie that you will cook further, like a quiche. Bake for 60 to 75 minutes if making a crust for a pie that will need no further baking.
For mini heart ramekin pies:
Make about 1/3 of recipe? Bake at 425F for 30-35min?

recipes.name: tortillas
recipes.label: tortillas
directions: Mix dry in a bowl. Stir in wet. Mix, then knead til smooth.
Separate into 9 balls, lightly flatten with palm. Cover and let rest 15min to up to 2 hrs.
Nonstick pan on med heat. (Maybe try slightly above med heat?)
Flatten each ball with rolling pin. Should be pretty thin and translucent!
Heat each tortilla. First side for 1 min (should have lightly browned spots. if not, heat is too low. if it takes less than 1 min, heat is too high). Other side for 15-30 sec.
Keep warm til ready to serve. Store room temp for 24hrs or fridge for up to 1 week.

recipes.name: pizza
recipes.label: pizza
directions: Mix flour + salt + yeast + water. Knead til well combined. Let dough rest for 15 min. Knead again to smooth ball.
Let rest and rise 3-4 hrs at room temp or 8-24 hrs in fridge (if so, take out 30-45 min before shaping).
Can also rise for a bit room temp, then the rest in fridge. If 4+ hrs, just rise it in fridge.
Can freeze for a month; just punch down, roll into ball, tight wrap. Let thaw in fridge overnight and then roll out.
475F 10 min.

recipes.name: easy_milk_bread
recipes.label: easy milk bread
directions: Melt butter and mix with milk. Into room temp mix, add yeast and let rest.
In large bowl, mix flour, sugar, salt. Stir in boiling water. Add in egg and milk mix.
Mix til shaggy mass. Start kneading in bowl. Will be messy at first, but will get smoother as you knead.
Knead til windowpane test passes (take piece about size of golf ball, stretch it out between fingers and thumbs. if stretches without breaking, good)
Shape into ball, place in lightly floured bowl, cover, rest til tripled in size (~1hr)
Punch down and divide into three pieces.
On a floured surface, roll each piece into a tall oval. Fold the oval sides into the middle (kind of looks like a cannoli). Roll up (from top to bottom)
Place in nonstick pan (9x5). Cover and let proof til doubled (40 min~1hr)
Bake at 355F 20 min, up to 25min if needed
Note, for coffee bread: 4 tbsp per 250g flour

recipes.name: pizza_rolls
recipes.label: pizza rolls
directions: Mix flour, salt, sugar, yeast well. Crack egg in and mix well.
Combine milk and butter, then add to dough. Mix dough well, then cover and rest til doubled (~30 min)
Roll out dough into a rectangle. Spread filling on top with some cheese.
Slice like kimbap, put into a baking tray (sliced sides up), and let rise 10-12 min.
Top with green onions and rest of cheese.
355F for 18 min.

recipes.name: pita
recipes.label: pita

items.name: flour
items.note: approx, for extra dusting as needed

directions: Makes 3 pitas
In lukewarm water, stir yeast and sugar til dissolved. Add 1/6c (20g) flour and whisk together.
Let sit in warm place and get bubbly, turning into a loose sponge (~15min)
Add salt, olive, and almost all remaining flour. (Use 80g. Keep ~20g for dusting)
Stir til shaggy mass. Dust with a little flour. Knead mix for about 1 min.
Add more flour. Knead on dusted surface for a couple min til smooth
Cover and let dough rest 10 min. Knead again couple more min. (Dough should be lil moist, but dust a bit more if need.)
Let dough rise in greased bowl til doubled (~1hr). After rising, can place dough in fridge til cooking time!
Divide into 3 pieces. Knead and flatten. Doesn't have to be too thin!
Cook on stainless steel skillet on med high heat. Should be ~30sec-1min on each side.

recipes.name: dinner_rolls
recipes.label: dinner rolls
directions: Makes 3 rolls:
Mix water + butter + yeast, let rest.
Combine about 75% of flour, sugar, salt, add liquids and mix well.
Add rest of flour and combine til smooth. Use more flour as needed (60g needed can use up to 75g)
Cover, let rest 10 min. Divide into balls, place in greased pan, cover and rise til doubled.
Bake at 375F 11-14 minutes or til lightly browned. Brush tops with a little melted butter. (1tsp for 3 rolls)

recipes.name: brioche
recipes.label: brioche
directions: Mix warm water + yeast, let rest ~5min.
In big bowl, melt butter. Add and mix in honey, egg, and yeast mix.
In smaller bowl, mix flour + salt. Add the dry to wet, mix well with spoon.
Cover, let sit 2 hrs room temp, then chill overnight or up to 5 days. Can also be used as soon as dough has chilled.
If making red bean brioche, use 6 tbsp of paste.
Place into parchment lined 8x4 loaf pan. Cover, let rise 1.5-2hrs or til doubled.
Bake at 350F for 30-40 min.

recipes.name: crusty_bread_rolls
recipes.label: crusty bread rolls
directions: Makes 12 rolls.
Make starter by mixing starter ingredients together til smooth, covering, and letting rest room temp overnight.
Combine all dough ingredients + starter. Mix and knead til soft, somewhat smooth dough--cohesive but surface may be bit rough and dough may stick to bowl tiny bit.
Cover, let dough rise for 3 hours. (1 hr into rise, gently deflate it and turn it over, and let rest the remaining 2 hrs).
Divide dough into 12 pieces, shape into balls, place on parchment lined baking sheet. Cover and let rise 1-2 hrs (puffy but not doubled!--will flatten as rise, dont worry)
Cover and chill for 2-3 hrs. Don't worry if flat, they will puff in oven. Optional egg wash of frothy whisked egg white + water.
Slash 1/4" deep cut across tops. Immediately put into oven.
Bake at 425F 20-25min til deep golden brown. Remove from oven and let cool, or for better crunch, open oven door and let rolls cool in turned-off open-door oven.
(For larger rolls, divide the dough into 8 pieces, rather than 12. Bake for a minute or two longer.)

recipes.name: rice_flour_roti
recipes.label: rice flour roti
directions: Makes 6 rotis.
Boil water in a covered pot. Once boiling, mix in flour and salt and stir until crumbly mass.
Take off heat, leave pot covered for 10 min.
Knead together and roll into 6 balls. Flatten each ball and fry on med-med high heat non-stick skillet.

recipes.name: chai_latte
recipes.label: chai latte
directions: For 1 serving: 1 cup of water, 1/2 tsp of chai spice mix, 1 cup of milk, 2 tea bags (or 2 tsp of loose leaf), 2 tsp of sugar.
Grind spices.
Bring water to boil med high. Add spices, boil for 10 min on med low heat.
Add tea, sugar, milk. Bring to boil, take off heat, serve.

recipes.name: banana_bread
recipes.label: banana bread
directions: Mash ripe bananas with fork til smooth. Stir melted butter into bananas.
Mix in baking soda and salt. Stir in sugar, beaten egg, vanilla, Mix in flour.
350F, greased 8x4 pan, 50min-1hr (til toothpick test clean). Cool completely before slicing.

recipes.name: macaroons
recipes.label: macaroons
directions: Mix coconut, milk, vanilla.
Beat egg whites and salt into soft peaks. Gently fold egg whites into coconut mix.
Scoop ~2 tbsp at a time (makes 16). Bake at 325F for 25 min or until golden brown.
Immediately remove from baking sheet to cool. Dip in chocolate and set for 1-2 hours once cool.

recipes.name: thick_texas_cookies
recipes.label: thick texas cookies
directions: Add butter and sugars to bowl and cream until light and fluffy.
Add egg and vanilla and mix. Add dry ingredients and mix. It's okay that it's crumbly.
Add chocolate chips. Mold onto baking sheet and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Bake for 18 minutes at 375F.
Let cookies cool on baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer them and let cool to room temperature.

recipes.name: edible_cookie_dough
recipes.label: edible cookie dough
directions: Heat treat flour (bake at 350F for 7 min)
Whip butter, sugars, salt. Mix in milk and vanilla.
Fold in flour and choc chips.
For chocolate version: replace 10g of flour with 10g of cocoa powder.

recipes.name: hong_kong_egg_tart
recipes.label: hong kong egg tart
directions: Makes 8 tarts.
For pastry:
Combine flour, salt, sugar. Add butter and break up roughly with fingers. Make sure butter chunks are visible in dough.
Add water and bring dough together. Cover dough and refrigerate for 20 min.
Knead dough into a rectangle on floured surface. Roll dough away from body (not back and forth) into a 8x20 inch rectangle. Don't overwork! Butter should still be visible.
Fold top third of dough down to center, then the bottom third up and over that. Give the dough a 90deg turn and roll out to 3x the length. Do the same folding.
Cover and chill for 30 min.
For custard:
Microwave water + sugar til dissolved.
Whisk egg, milk, vanilla. Mix water in little bits at a time. Strain.
425F for 15 min. If it starts bubbling up, crack open oven.

recipes.name: easy_madeleines
recipes.label: easy madeleines
directions: Makes 10 cookies
Beat eggs + sugar until pale yellow. Add zest.
Whisk together dry ingredients add to the egg mixture gently, until just combined. Then add melted butter.
Refrigerate batter at least 1 hour (or overnight).
Preheat oven to 350F. Grease madeleine pan. Spoon 1 tbsp or ~16.7g of batter into the center of the well and don’t touch it.
Bake 8 mins until slightly golden around edges and centers look set and slight hump has appeared.
Allow to cool slightly and remove from the tin and allow to cool.
Batter can be made 2 days ahead, baked ones last in fridge 2-3 days.

recipes.name: madeleines
recipes.label: madeleines
directions: Makes 9-10 cookies
Melt butter, then cool to room temp for 8 min.
During those 8 min, beat egg and sugar until thick, pale, and forms ribbons. Beat in zest and vanilla until combined.
Whisk flour, baking powder, salt in small bowl. Gently fold into egg mix. Tip: fold in half first, then the rest. Be careful and delicate!!
Stir 1/4c of batter into butter, will take about a min to incorporate. Stir that into the rest of the batter.
Chill for 30-60 min.
Grease up pan with melted butter.
Preheat oven to 350F.
Spoon about 1 tbsp (~21.7g) batter into each scallop. Bake for 10 min, maybe less.
Note: easy madeleines look less saturated in color slightly. This one: airier, more buttery, lighter, crispier. Difference is barely noticeable.

recipes.name: mini_cheesecake
recipes.label: mini cheesecake
directions: Mix cream cheese and sugar til smooth and creamy.
Beat egg yolk til incorporated. Beat in vanilla (and melted chocolate, if using)
Divide batter into two 6-oz or three 4-oz ramekins.
Bake 18-20 min at 350F. Ok if they jiggle.
Cool completely. Chill overnight, or 4+ hours.

recipes.name: apple_pie
recipes.label: apple pie
directions: Combine flour + salt. Add butter and cut in with knife.
When butter is the size of peas, use hands to squeeze dough for a few min.
When dough stays in a clump after you squeeze it, place in fridge for 10 min.
Add vinegar and 2 tbsp of water. Stir with fork til mixture comes together. Add more water as needed, but dont use more than 5 tbsp water total.
Shape dough into flat disk, wrap, and fridge for 30 min.
Toss apples in spices + sugar.
Roll out crust. Press down apples when adding to prevent air gaps.
Bake at 425F on lowest rack for 20 min. Lower temp to 350F and bake on middle rack for 15-20 min til filling is bubbling. Cool for 2 hours.

recipes.name: carrot_cake
recipes.label: carrot cake
directions: Makes 5-6 cupcakes, or 1/4 a 9x13 cake pan
Grate carrots.
Mix flour, baking soda, salt, spices.
Whisk oil, sugars, vanilla. Whisk egg one at a time.
Combine dry and wet. Add carrots and nuts.
Bake at 350F 30-40 min. Cool in pan for 15 min, then cool on rack. (If cupcake, bake for 14-18 min)
Frosting: whisk cream cheese and butter til smooth. Mix sugar, vanilla, and salt til smooth.

recipes.name: hamantaschen
recipes.label: hamantaschen

items.name: egg
items.note: whisked (use half for dough, half for wash)

directions: Makes about 17 cookies.
Cream sugar + butter til light and fluffy.
Add egg, vanilla, zest. Beat til well mixed.
Sift in flour and salt. Mix until crumbly dough.
Knead and add water (tsp at a time) til dough is smooth and slightly tacky (not sticky), with consistency that is right for rolling out.
Form dough into disk, wrap, chill for 3+ hours. Let rest room temp for 30 min before rolling.
Prepare filling and preheat to 350F.
Roll dough to 1/4 inch thin. Lift dough, lightly flour bottom, flip, roll out to 1/8 inch. (Can keep at 1/4 if you like it Thick..)
Cut out ideally 17 circles total. 3 inch in diameter
Egg wash the outside/rims of the top of the circles. Teaspoon of filling each.
Fold into a pinwheel triangle shape: each of the three flaps should overlap and underlap its neighbor!! Pinch corners to seal.
Bake for 10-25 min, probably more like 15-20 min.

recipes.name: chocolate_hummus
recipes.label: chocolate hummus
directions: Blend in food processor.

recipes.name: chocolate_chili_cookies
recipes.label: chocolate chili cookies
directions: In small bowl, whisk flour, cocoa, salt, ginger, cinnamon, clove, and chili.
In another small bowl, stir baking soda into warm water until dissolved. Set aside.
In large bowl, beat butter and sugar until smooth and creamy, ~1-2 min.
Beat in honey and vanilla until smooth.
Gradually add half of flour mix. Add baking soda mix, then remaining flour. Stir in choc chips.
Chill until firm, ~30 min. Make 4 cookie balls, roll each in sugar.
Bake 13-15 min at 325F. Will be soft, cool on baking sheet for 5 min.

recipes.name: zucchini_bread
recipes.label: zucchini bread
directions: One 8x4 loaf or 12 muffins (fill muffins all the way to top). Grease tins.
Optional 1 cup of fillings (nuts, chocolate, raisins...)
Whisk flour, baking powder, baking soda, spices, and filling in large bowl til thoroughly combined.
Separate medium bowl, whisk oil, sugars, egg, vanilla, and zucchini til combined.
Pour wet ingredients into dry, gently whisk til just combined, don't overmix.
Bake 45-55 min at 375F. Toothpick should come out mostly clean. Let cool before slicing.
If muffins: Bake at 425F for 5 min, reduce to 350F with muffins still in oven for 13-16 min or until toothpick clean. Cool in tin for 5 min, then wire rack.
Room temp 3-4 days, fridge 1 week, freezer up to 3 month.

recipes.name: cinnamon_rolls
recipes.label: cinnamon rolls

items.name: flour
items.note: originally 2.25 cup. try a little less

items.name: sugar
items.note: or 1.5 tsp for the less sweet danish version

directions: Microwave milk + butter til ~95F. Mix in sugar + yeast and let sit til foamy.
Add egg + salt (+ cardamom) and mix.
Gradually add in flour. Mix til smooth dough.
Knead into a ball and rest in greased covered bowl for 1-2hr (or til doubled).
Roll dough out into 9x12 rect, spread cinnamon butter mix on top.
Roll dough up tight into 12 inch log. Cut into 5-6 rolls.
Cover tight and chill 8-12 hours. Before baking, remove from fridge and let rise til puffy (~1-2hr)
Bake 25min 375F. Tent with foil hallfway through to prevent over browning. Cool for 10 min, then ice. Lasts 3 days room temp.

recipes.name: chocolate_babka
recipes.label: chocolate babka

items.name: chocolate_chips
items.note: up to 60g if you're feeling luxurious

directions: Heat up milk + vanilla + nutmeg. Add yeast to milk and let bubble.
Warm egg to room temp. Heat up butter a little.
Flour, sugar, salt, mix til combined.
Add egg + milk mix to dry. Mix til dough comes together. (Use fork, will be sticky)
Add butter gradually, mix til incorporated.
Keep using fork, will be sticky for a whie. Mix til dough is completely smooth, elastic, shiny, pulls away from sides.
Dough in greased cover bowl. Rest for half a day or overnight.
Grease 9x4 in loaf pan. Line bottom with parchment paper.
Make filling by whisking everything into a paste.
Roll out dough into rect (15x11?), long side closest to you. Spread filling, sprinkle choc chips on top.
Shape dough: Roll tight. Fridge/freezer for 10 min to set, cut in half lengthwise, braid from center.
Put dough in loaf pan. Cover loosely, rise for 1-1.5 hr. Optionally, rise overnight, then take out to get to room temp for an hour before baking.
Bake 40-50 min 350F. 45 min is definitely good. Experiment.
Brush with sugar syrup IMMEDIATELY after out of oven.
Cool til warm, remove from pans, cool completely before serving.

recipes.name: sliced_sweet_potato_pie
recipes.label: sliced sweet potato pie
directions: Pair with 1 9-inch pie crust.
Peel and slice sweet potatoes (1/4 in thick). Boil on high heat til tender
Mix sugar, flour, salt, spices together. Toss sweet potatoes in spices.
Place sweet potatoes in crust. Dot butter on top of sweet potatoes.
Bake 350F for 1hr. Let cool completely.

recipes.name: steamed_custard_buns
recipes.label: steamed custard buns

items.name: water
items.note: room temp; to sub cold milk

directions: Makes 6 buns.
Mix flour, yeast, sugar, salt, oil. Add room temp water (slowly add more if too dry. potentially up to 90ml). Mix til rough dough.
Cover, let rest 30min. Then, knead 10min more. If hard to knead, just let rest. Do one more 15min rest before shaping.
Divide dough into smaller pieces, rolling into very smooth tall balls (smooth top with milk/water if desired).

Place each ball on parchment paper. Let rise only once, to 50% larger (don't let double in size!) Done proving when poking dough leaves indent that bounces back slowly (not enough if bounce back fast). (very roughly 30min room temp)
Bring steamer water to boil, lower the heat to MEDIUM. Place buns 1 inch apart, close lid and crack open about a 1/4 inch gap.
Steam 15 min for no filling, 18 min for filling.
After steaming, turn off heat. Do NOT open lid and let sit for 5 min.
Good filling is 1 small sweet potato (175g raw) steamed and mashed, or 200g red bean,...
Custard steps:
Mix egg + sugar + cornstarch/flour well.
Put pot with butter + milk on low/med heat, heat up til just melted.
Pour milk into egg. Pour all into pot. Heat over low heat, stir frequently, and take off just when thickened.
Freeze for 20 min. Shape, then freeze for 3+hr.

recipes.name: sweet_potato_cake
recipes.label: sweet potato cake

items.name: flour
items.note: combine with cornstarch to sub cake flour

items.name: cornstarch
items.note: combine with flour to sub cake flour

directions: Cake (18 cm genoise):
Melt butter, milk, vanilla gently (ideally over hot water bath)
Separate egg white + yolk.
Add sugar to yolk. Mix well, til pale ribbons.
Whip egg whites + sugar (add sugar in 3 batches) til soft peaks.
Carefully fold meringue into yolk mix, gradually.
Sift in flour. Fold gently.
Gently mix butter mix into batter. (Either add bit of batter into butter, mix, then combine with rest of batter; or pour butter carefully around edge of batter and fold inwards carefully)
Filling:
Boil sweet potatoes til soft. Peel and mash while hot.
Add sugar, mix well. Add cream, mix well.
Frosting:
Important to start with cold cream! Can even chill the bowl if you want.
In a large bowl, whip the whipping cream. When thicker, add powdered sugar. Whip til medium peaks.
Sugar syrup:
Microwave water + sugar. Doesn't have to be exactly this amount.
Assembly:
Make genoise crumbs from leftover cake
Place Genoise slice, brush with syrup, spread filling, repeat.
Ice entire cake with frosting and cover with genoise crumbs.

recipes.name: meringue
recipes.label: meringue

items.name: sugar
items.note: ideally ground or superfine, esp for pavlova

items.name: vinegar_white
items.note: if making pavlova; ACV ok too

directions: Preheat oven ahead of time, because meringue must go in immediately.
Cold eggs are easier to separate. Room temp egg whites are easier to whip.
Whip egg white til soft peaks.
Add sugar 1 tbsp at a time.
Beat til stiff peaks.
Gently fold in flavorings if desired. If making pavlova, fold in vanilla/vinegar/cornstarch mix now.
Shape appropriately
Bake 225F 1hr (or 215F for 60-90min?).
Turn off oven and let meringue sit for 1-2 hrs.
Store cookies in covered container room temp for a few days, don't put in fridge.

recipes.name: choux
recipes.label: choux

items.name: flour
items.note: 1.175oz (1.25oz for firmer choux, like eclair) originally

items.name: egg
items.note: 2oz weight with shell; ideally 1 large egg

directions: Makes 4-5 eclairs
Beat eggs together into measuring glass for easy pour.
Med heat, cold water + salt + butter. Stir occasionally.
When water start to boil (butter should be melted!), remove from stove (keep stove on) and add flour all at once.
Stir vigorously until flour is all incorporated. Wooden spoon recoommended.
Place back on med heat, cook dough for 1-3 min until panade is 175F. (There should be a thin film on the bottom of saucepan once ready.)
Let dough cool to 145F. Add vanilla now if using.
Add eggs "one at a time" and whisk well after each. Stop when consistency is right (you might not use all the eggs). Should be glossy and smooth and pass the finger and spatula v test.
Mist baking sheet with water. Pipe or spoon.
15min at 450F and then 325F for 20min. Or 10min at 425F and then 325F for 30 min. Or 375F for 30-40min. Should be golden brown. Make sure to prick once out of oven. Cool.
Cut butter into pieces.
Bring milk and vanilla just to a boil. Stir to prevent film.
Whisk sugar, cornstarch, salt. Add yolk and whisk til smooth, pale, and creamy.
Gradually, to prevent curdle, add hot milk into egg mix and whisk constantly till all incorporated.
Bring mix back to stove and bring to bowl while whisking constantly. Mix till thick and puddinglike.
Whisk in vanilla if using. Quickly add in butter cubes and whisk into custard til fully incorporated. Cover with plastic and chill for 30+min.

recipes.name: red_bean_paste
recipes.label: red bean paste
directions: Rinsed beans + sugar into pot with water 1 inch above.
Bring to boil, reduce, and simmer for 1 hr.
After this point, water should just cover beans.
Makes about 20 tbsp (1 tbsp = 18g)

recipes.name: queijadas
recipes.label: queijadas
directions: Mix all smoothly. 325F 45min. or 400F 30min.

recipes.name: clafoutis
recipes.label: clafoutis
directions: Put fruit into buttered pan.
Whisk eggs + sugar til smooth. Whisk in salt + flour til smooth. Whisk in milk + extracts til smooth.
Pour batter over fruit.
350F, 45min (til lightly brown and tester mostly clean)

recipes.name: tofu_pudding
recipes.label: tofu pudding
directions: Blend and chill.

recipes.name: manju
recipes.label: manju
directions: https://youtu.be/21PrqMtd5D0
Makes 15-18 manju
Mix condensed milk + yolk, add optional pinch of salt. Sift in flour and baking powder. Mix well.
Cover and chill for 30min. Fill with white bean. (Keep dough covered to stay moist while shaping!)
Bake at 350F 20min.
One 15oz can beans makes ~260g paste.
Remove bean skins. Place beans in pot with 1.5 cup water and cook over med high heat for 10 min.
Then, reduce heat to very low, and simmer beans til soft and fluffy. Mash with spoon til mashed potatoes.
Add sugar, salt, vanilla. Turn heat to med high, stir 2-3 min to thicken. Turn heat off and cool down.
Combine all dough ingredients and mix well til smooth.
Shape and fill. Makes ~12 manju. Bake 350F for 20min.

recipes.name: thai_tea
recipes.label: thai tea
directions: For first option, use 160g ice.
Bring 6 tea bags, water, sugar, spices to a simmer over med heat. Reduce heat to low and let simmer for 3-5 min.
Strain and pour tea over ice. Top with milk.

recipes.name: chocolate_cake
recipes.label: chocolate cake
directions: Grease and flour 9x5 (or 8x4?) pan.
Stir sugar, flour, cocoa, baking soda, baking powder, salt in large bowl.
Add eggs, buttermilk, coffee, oil, vanilla; beat on med speed with mixer 2 min. Pour batter evenly into prepared pan.
Bake 30-40 min or til tester comes out clean. Cool 10 min, remove from pan, then cool completely.
If muffins, makes 12: 350F 18-20 min or til tester clean, then cool in pan for 20 min, remove and cool completely.

recipes.name: taiwanese_castella
recipes.label: taiwanese_castella
directions: Heat milk + butter/oil til just melted.
Sift cake flour into bowl, pour hot milk in and mix well til sticky. Add yolks, salt, vanilla and whisk til smooth (careful not to make too big air bubbles)
Start preheating oven at this point (300F), and heating up hot water (176F, not boiling!!) for water bath.
Beat egg whites til foamy. Gently add sugar onto sides and beat on low speed. Once all sugar in, beat on high speed til SOFT peaks.
Add 1/3 of meringue into batter, whisk thoroughly. Gently fold in another 1/3 til no white streaks. Fold last 1/3 in very gently til just combined.
Pour batter into parchment lined pan. Shake pan to spread evenly and tap on counter twice to release big bubbles.
Place pan in 1-inch water bath. Bake on a lower rack, 45-55min or til top is brown, spongy, and dry.
Remove immediately from pan and peel off paper to prevent shrinkage.

recipes.name: baci_di_dama
recipes.label: baci di dama
directions: Toast hazelnuts at 320F for 20min til golden and fragrant. Rub to get loose skins off (don't worry about skins that stay). Cool completely and food process til like sand.
Mix hazelnuts, sifted flour, butter, sugar well til dough is smooth.
Divide dough into 4 pieces, roll into log, and chill for 1hr.
Working on one log at a time to keep cold, cut off small pieces (~5-8g) and roll into tiny balls. Place balls onto lined baking sheet, slightly spaced apart, and chill for 1hr.
Bake at 320F 15 min til set and golden. Cool completely. Sandwich with melted chocolate.
Stores for 1 week at room temp.

recipes.name: oatmeal_raisin_cookies
recipes.label: oatmeal raisin cookies
directions: Whisk flour, cinnamon, baking soda, salt. Cream butter and sugars.
Add egg and vanilla and mix well. Slowly add flour mix. Fold in oats and raisins gently.
Chill for 20 min. Makes ~8 cookies. Bake at 350F 10-12 min.

recipes.name: thick_cookies_part_2
recipes.label: thick cookies part 2
directions: Combine butter, sugars, vanilla, salt, baking powder, baking soda, nutmeg and mix. Beat til soft, fluffy, and pale.
Add egg, mix til incorporated. Add flour, mix til incorporated. Add fillings, mix til incorporated.
Divide into 4 smooth balls (approx 170g each). Wrap and chill 12+hrs before baking, up to 1 week.
Bake at 350F 22min (internal temp 175-185F).

recipes.name: buttermilk_pie
recipes.label: buttermilk pie
directions: Pair with 1 9-inch pie crust.
Make buttermilk by replacing 1tbsp w vinegar. So, 1/16 of liquid amount is vinegar, 15/16 of liquid amount is milk.
Beat eggs til frothy. Add butter, sugar, flour, beat til smooth. Stir in buttermilk, vanilla, lemon juice, nutmeg. Pour into pie shell.
Maybe blindbake crust a bit
Bake 350F for 50-60min, til center is firm. Let cool completely.
